title: How much did you know about Eva before you started watching
display_title: How much did you know about Eva before you started watching
wikitext:
[]: How much did you know about Eva before you started watching?             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Mon Oct 24, 2011 4:14 am Well before I started watching Eva, I knew it was called Neon Genesis Evangelion, the last two episodes had basically nothing to do with the rest of the series, the series ended by a boy whom I presumed was the main character being congratulated, and that there was a movie which was meant as the original ending.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Oct 24, 2011 5:20 am I was told I would rage at the ending, by two friends who egged me into watching it. And, based on the context of the situation, I knew that the ending wouldn't give any answers. Joke was on them, though, I loved the ending. And I found out about EoE and watched that too, and neither of them had ever heard of it till I told them.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Oct 24, 2011 7:53 am I knew absolutely nothing . The series kind of shocked me at first. I remember having to stop at episode 23, because of the fish tank Rei's. keep in mind I was 12, also the castellano dub was awesome

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Mon Oct 24, 2011 8:48 am Lets see... I ended up seeing EoE first while seriously sleep-deprived, from grainy VHS fansub. Needless to say, I couldn't make heads or tails about it. I mostly forgot everything about it. (Hey, when I say sleep deprived, I'm talking about "30 hours awake streak".) Then I watched NGE from grainy digital fansubs with the knowledge "this is hot shit in the anime neighborhood right now" (it was -98 or -99 then). And remembering "hey, that redhead died in the film and then she was alive again thanks to some nirvana rebirth stuff? OK..."

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Mon Oct 24, 2011 8:56 am Only what little information there was from the Amazon page for the Perfect Collection version (the original box set) and the user reviews, so not much really. Like most people, I expected yet another (although very highly rated and recommended) mecha anime. Hoo boy, had I only known then...

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Mon Oct 24, 2011 9:54 am I knew nothing all the information I had was a commercial calling 2.22 perfection. Then one impulse buy later I am a huge fan of Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 24, 2011 11:06 am I thought it was called "Neo Genesis E-vanj-e-lon", and that Shinji was a girl. I also thought it was an action show.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Mon Oct 24, 2011 12:12 pm Absolutely nothing. I didn't even mean to watch it, really. I saw a wallpaper for Evangelion 1.0 on some desktop wallpaper website and thought "That's a cool mech and interesting background, guess I'll watch it" and since I didn't know it was of Rebuild I went and watched NGE instead. I'm glad I did, as I don't know what I would have thought of NGE if I'd gotten used to a big-ass budget, low-ass characters version of it. I probably wouldn't have even bothered trying to understand it. And I used to think it was pronounced "Ee-van-jel-ee-on".

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Oct 24, 2011 1:17 pm Pretty much everything including spoilers and the ending(s). Then again, I only got into it eighteen months ago so I was late to the party anyways. I pronounce it Ee-van-ge-li-on.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 24, 2011 2:03 pm Not a goddamned thing. The first episode just appeared on Adult Swim one night, I was like, "sure, whatever."

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Mon Oct 24, 2011 2:48 pm Not a goddamned thing. The first episode just appeared on Adult Swim one night, I was like, "sure, whatever."

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Mon Oct 24, 2011 3:03 pm I knew almost nothing. I had heard about eva all over the place, so I read some of the manga and I had no idea what it was all about, but I picked it up as I went. The main reason I joined the forum was that I did not understand the last few episodes of NGE, and I did not understand Death and Rebirth either.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Oct 24, 2011 3:35 pm I knew it from AMV Hell 4, but I didn't know it's name until earlier this year.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Oct 24, 2011 4:06 pm I knew that some guy would be killed by an unnamed assassin, it had a weird sounding name, some kid whacks off to a corpse, orange juice played a large role in the ending, and that it was based on the book of Genesis. So, I was wrong about most of it.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Mon Oct 24, 2011 5:26 pm No idea, never got myself much into anime until I saw that very first episode on a bootlegger's TV while window shopping at a mall 13 years ago.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Mon Oct 24, 2011 6:54 pm I heard about it on deviantart and various other blogs. Seeing it that it was so popular, I researched into it a lot before I actually started watching the show; and even before that, I read fanfictions, looked on wikis, read reviews, everything before I had the courage to watch it, and considering all the mindfucks and WTF moments in the movie, it really payed off.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 24, 2011 8:12 pm I knew the creative team from Nadia: The Secret of Blue Water was working on it, which was so delightfully charming and witty back when Anno was directing it. Surely his Neon Genesis Evangelion show would be similar, especially since he directed it right after Nadia, and was therefore "still in the zone". Why did Anno suddenly seem to hate me? WHY???

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Mon Oct 24, 2011 8:50 pm There was a robot boy in an ad in Wizard. I think this was shortly after Wolverine got his adamantium ripped out?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Oct 24, 2011 9:17 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: and that Shinji was a girl.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Mon Oct 24, 2011 9:24 pm Just that it was advertised as a giant robot show.
summary: []: How much did you know about Eva before you started watching?             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Mon Oct 24, 2011 4:14 am Well before I started watching Eva, I knew it was called Neon Genesis Evangelion, the last two episodes had basically nothing to do with the rest of the series, the series ended by a boy whom I presumed was the main character being congratulated, and that there was a movie which was meant as the original ending.
